details.output: Added a Stop button to every active Agents row and unified it on POST /api/processes/:id/stop; managed, interactive, and chat agent stops now terminate or close the selected agent, and Goal-linked stops persist and return the Goal as cancelled. Updated process intent and added UI/server regression coverage. Verification: cargo test --lib: 455 passed, 0 failed; node --test tests/*.test.js: 36 passed, 0 failed; cargo check --all-targets: passed; cargo fmt -- --check: passed; git diff --check: passed.

failed_actions.message: The web route directly orchestrates chat lookup, process termination, and Goal cancellation instead of delegating this product behavior to a shared capability, making the surface non-thin.
failed_actions.rule: Respect the Foundation-Capabilities-Surfaces boundary.

failed_actions.message: Stop-agent-and-cancel-Goal semantics exist only in the web server route; the shared process service still performs process-only signaling, so there is no authoritative implementation of the combined operation.
failed_actions.rule: Compose existing capabilities with one authoritative implementation per concern.

failed_actions.message: The stop path uses FileProcessSupervisor::signal, which removes the process record immediately after requesting termination, then marks the Goal cancelled without confirming the process exited. A termination-resistant agent can remain alive but untracked under a cancelled Goal.
failed_actions.rule: Keep long-running work supervised with explicit status, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: The tests cover a cooperative sleep process only and assert Goal cancellation before checking eventual exit; there is no failure-path evidence for a process that ignores or delays termination, despite changing process cancellation and recovery semantics.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to architectural and operational risk.

failed_actions.message: The browser/API path now cancels a linked Goal, while CLI system ps --stop still invokes the shared signal-only service and does not cancel the Goal or handle synthetic chat-agent processes, producing inconsistent process-stop semantics across surfaces.
failed_actions.rule: Preserve one capability across UI, CLI, API, and MCP.

message: Workflow failed: Agent stopping is surface-specific and can cancel a Goal before confirming process exit.

details.error: Agent stopping is surface-specific and can cancel a Goal before confirming process exit.

details.output: Committed reviewable Round 2 candidate ea2103e3. Added one shared product process-control capability used by web/API, CLI system ps --stop, browser Agents controls, and MCP; it supports managed, interactive, chat, and synthetic agents, persists exact OS PID identity evidence, retains supervision records until confirmed exit, cancels linked Goals only after exit, and returns causal retained-evidence failures for resistance, delay, signal failure, registry replacement, and identity mismatch. Every active Agents row now has a process-specific Stop button. Focused verification: cargo test --lib process_control: 4 passed, 0 failed; web API managed/synthetic stop: 1 passed, 0 failed; CLI system process stop: 1 passed, 0 failed. Initial cargo test --lib exposed one plain-terminal classification regression (458 passed, 1 failed); narrowed agent classification and verified that test 1 passed, 0 failed; clean full rerun: 459 passed, 0 failed. node --test tests/*.test.js: 37 passed, 0 failed. cargo check --all-targets: passed. cargo fmt -- --check: passed. git diff --check and git diff --cached --check: passed. Rejected a1f6c2e6 and its Round 1 worktree remain preserved and separate.

failed_actions.message: For legacy or recovered process records lacking the new identity file, stop-time identity creation trusts the process currently occupying the recorded PID. After PID reuse, Refine can terminate an unrelated process and cancel the linked Goal without detecting an ownership mismatch.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Process stopping ignores the recorded execution_id, round, and workflow claim, then unconditionally cancels the Goal by ID. Stopping a stale process from an older execution can therefore cancel newer work that currently owns the same Goal.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: Tests cover mismatches only when identity evidence already exists and cover only one current execution. They do not exercise legacy missing-identity PID reuse or stale-execution concurrency, despite changing process ownership, persistence, cancellation, and recovery semantics.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

details.error: Process ownership migration and Goal-execution coordination remain unsafe.

details.output: Committed reviewable recovery c9a92ee5 on top of preserved Round 2 candidate ea2103e3. PID identity is now created only at a process's first registration; legacy or recovered records missing registration-time identity fail closed without signaling, synthesizing evidence, or cancelling a Goal. Workflow agent metadata now records claim_id with execution_id and round_idx, and the shared process-control capability validates the exact claim, execution, current Goal round, and absence of competing active claims both before termination and before Goal cancellation. Added focused success, missing-identity/PID-reuse, stale-execution concurrency, stale-round, mismatch, and resistant-process coverage plus the affected process intent update. Verification: cargo test --lib process_control: 8 passed, 0 failed; cargo test --lib subprocess: 10 passed, 0 failed; cargo test --lib agent_sessions: 3 passed, 0 failed; focused web API stop: 1 passed, 0 failed; focused CLI stop: 1 passed, 0 failed; workflow metadata: 1 passed, 0 failed; focused browser process UI: 4 passed, 0 failed; final cargo test --lib: 463 passed, 0 failed; node --test tests/*.test.js: 37 passed, 0 failed; cargo check --all-targets: passed; cargo fmt --all -- --check: passed; git diff --check, git diff --cached --check, and git show --check: passed. Two earlier full-library attempts exposed a transient interactive-terminal status failure at 462 passed, 1 failed; its isolated rerun passed 1/1 and the final unchanged-source full rerun passed 463/463.

failed_actions.message: If the post-exit ownership check fails, the process has already exited and recover() has removed its registry and identity evidence, but the returned error incorrectly says termination was not requested and omits the confirmed-exit result. The partial outcome is therefore reported inaccurately without usable recovery evidence.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: The final claim and round validation and cancel_goal_summary are separate, unlocked operations. A new round and claim can acquire the Goal after validation but before the unconditional cancellation write, allowing a stop from the previous execution to cancel newer work.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The tests install stale claim or round state before stopping; none exercise ownership changing after confirmed process exit or between final validation and Goal cancellation, leaving the concurrency and partial-failure path unverified.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. details.output: Recovered preserved ea2103e3/c9a92ee5 onto current main and committed Round 3 as 2d0d226b. The shared process-stop capability now captures an exact Goal revision/status/round fence, holds cross-process workflow-state and Goal-mutation locks through final claim/execution/competing-owner validation and cancellation, prevents terminal Goal execution retries, reports confirmed process-exit cleanup truthfully, and durably retains post-exit partial-failure receipts with Goal-not-cancelled, cause, and supported recovery evidence. Added deterministic coverage for ownership changing after confirmed exit and for concurrent Round/retry attempts precisely between final validation and cancellation settlement; updated affected process intent. Verification: cargo test --lib process_control: 10 passed, 0 failed; subprocess: 10 passed, 0 failed; agent_sessions: 3 passed, 0 failed; work_items: 23 passed, 0 failed; focused web API: 1 passed, 0 failed; CLI: 1 passed, 0 failed; MCP: 1 passed, 0 failed; workflow metadata: 1 passed, 0 failed; focused browser: 4 passed, 0 failed; final cargo test --lib: 465 passed, 0 failed; final node --test tests/*.test.js: 37 passed, 0 failed; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; git diff --check, git diff --cached --check, and git show --check passed. An earlier full-library run found web_server_retries_workflow_executions returning 404 (464 passed, 1 failed); the target-less retry compatibility guard was corrected, its focused rerun passed, and the unchanged final full rerun passed 465/465.

failed_actions.message: Cancellation remains split between two incompatible implementations: WorkflowEngine::cancel signals the process and marks the claim cancelled, while FileProcessControlService confirms exit and cancels the Goal without settling the claim.
failed_actions.rule: Compose the existing Process, Agents, Workflow, Git, state, quality, governance, and evidence capabilities before creating new machinery. Each concern should have one authoritative implementation and source of truth unless the Goal explicitly proposes an architectural replacement.

failed_actions.message: After confirming exit, terminate_owned_and_confirm_exit calls recover before constructing the termination outcome. A cleanup failure therefore returns as an ordinary stop error without confirmed-exit details or a partial-outcome receipt; registry deletion may already have made the process undiscoverable before identity cleanup fails.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: The supposedly atomic settlement only validates the workflow claim; it never marks that claim cancelled. A successful stop can leave the claim running until the killed workflow worker records failure, producing inconsistent Goal and claim state and temporarily retaining capacity ownership.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The focused tests use synthetic processes and do not execute a real workflow worker through stop, assert final claim state, exercise the worker-failure race, or inject registry/identity cleanup failure. The atomic-settlement test leaves its claim running and checks only that its execution ID was unchanged.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. details.output: Committed d81385dc on Round 4 after preserving and replaying the Round 3 stack. WorkflowEngine::cancel now delegates to the authoritative FileProcessControlService; confirmed process exit is durably recorded before registry/identity cleanup; cleanup failures retain accurate partial receipts; exact claim cancellation, Goal cancellation, and capacity release settle under the workflow/Goal fences; terminal worker failure cannot overwrite cancelled claim/Goal state. Added real workflow-worker cancellation/race coverage, registry and identity cleanup fault injection, final claim/capacity assertions, and updated process intent. Verification: process_control 12 passed, 0 failed; real workflow worker 1 passed, 0 failed; subprocess 10 passed, 0 failed; agent_sessions 3 passed, 0 failed; web API 1 passed, 0 failed; CLI 1 passed, 0 failed; MCP 1 passed, 0 failed; workflow metadata 1 passed, 0 failed; browser process UI 4 passed, 0 failed; final cargo test --lib 468 passed, 0 failed; node --test tests/*.test.js 37 passed, 0 failed; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; git diff --check, git diff --cached --check, and git show --check passed. Earlier full-library attempts each exposed the same pre-existing intra-test quality-settings EOF race in one of two live-replenishment tests (467 passed, 1 failed); both isolated reruns passed, the overly broad timing change was narrowed, and the final unchanged-source default full run passed 468/468.

message: Workflow failed: Cancellation can bypass process exit and can persist inconsistent Goal, claim, and capacity state.

details.error: Cancellation can bypass process exit and can persist inconsistent Goal, claim, and capacity state.

details.output: Committed Round 5 as 18bac9080a09689263971e7ed648c6f516191e20. Target-bound cancellation now fails closed when no managed-process record exists; a shared spawn-to-registration fence prevents workers from starting under settled claims; exact claim, capacity, and Goal writes are journaled and rolled back together on failure; confirmed-exit receipts retain ownership and support retry recovery. Added real-worker pre-registration coverage, post-cancellation launch rejection, and injected failures after claim persistence, capacity release, and Goal persistence with exact state and recovery assertions; updated process intent and shared-capacity coverage. Focused results: process_control 14/14, real pre-registration worker 1/1, real worker settlement 1/1, workflow replenishment 2/2, subprocess 11/11, agent sessions 3/3, web API 1/1, CLI 1/1, MCP 1/1, workflow metadata 1/1, browser process UI 4/4. Initial full library run: 470 passed, 2 failed (obsolete zero-process cancellation expectation; known quality-settings EOF race); both isolated reruns passed. Two subsequent unchanged-source full library runs passed 472/472, including the final run. Final web suite passed 37/37; cargo check --all-targets, cargo fmt --all -- --check, git diff --check, git diff --cached --check, and git show --check passed. Worktree is clean; all prior candidate commits and worktrees were preserved.

failed_actions.message: The settlement journal has no replay path. If execution stops after claim persistence or capacity release but before Goal persistence, a retry sees the cancelled claim and returns already_cancelled without consulting the journal, leaving the Goal and capacity inconsistent and unrecoverable through the shared capability.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Successful settlement uses a WorkflowEngine without target-app context; persisting the cancelled claim therefore replaces the stored workflow policy with defaults, silently discarding configured limits, Node, provider, and target-app identity.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The tests cover in-process errors that execute rollback, but do not cover interruption and restart between durable writes or assert preservation of a non-default workflow policy, leaving both persistence defects unverified.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

A later full run exposed a new policy-test-only /proc disappearance race (473 passed, 1 failed); the unnecessary subprocess was removed from that success-only test while real restart tests retained process-exit coverage, focused reruns passed, and the final full run passed 474/474.
